summary.para: How DateTime values are framed in the emitted JavaScript.
Local time: new Date(y, m, d, ...) and getFullYear()/getMonth()/...
UTC: new Date(Date.UTC(y, m, d, ...)) and getUTCFullYear()/getUTCMonth()/...
Converts a C# lambda expression into an equivalent JavaScript expression string.
The CLR type the lambda produces.
How DateTime values are framed in the emitted JavaScript - local time or UTC.
Registry for mapping method calls the built-ins don't cover to JavaScript.
Registry for mapping constant types the built-ins don't cover to JavaScript.
Registry for mapping property reads the built-ins don't cover to JavaScript.
Registry for binding a parameter type to a host-side lookup instead of emitting it verbatim.
The emitted JavaScript - the whole reason to use this type.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.ExpressionCompiler.#ctor(System.Linq.Expressions.LambdaExpression)

summary.para: Creates a compiler for the given lambda using local-time date framing.

member.param: The expression to translate - usually a validation predicate. Its parameters become the free variables of the emitted JavaScript, so they determine what the client code reads from; its body is what gets translated.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.ExpressionCompiler.#ctor(System.Linq.Expressions.LambdaExpression,Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.DateTimeMode)

summary.para: Creates a compiler for the given lambda with an explicit date framing.

member.param: The expression to translate. Its parameters become the free variables of the emitted JavaScript; its body is what gets translated.

Sets ExpressionCompiler.DateMode . Choose the frame the host will bind JS-side dates in: DateTimeMode.Local emits local-time constructs, DateTimeMode.Utc emits UTC ones. A mismatch silently shifts every calendar-component comparison by the client's offset.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.ExpressionCompiler.AddConstant(System.Object)

summary.para: Renders a single value as the JavaScript literal the compiler would emit for it.

member.param: The value to render. Its runtime type selects the literal form (string, number, boolean, date, enum, ...); null renders as null. A type the built-ins don't recognize throws.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.ExpressionCompiler.FormatDateLiteral(System.DateTime,Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.DateTimeMode)

summary.para: Renders a fixed date as the JavaScript Date literal the compiler would emit for it.

member.param: The date to render. A midnight value emits a date-only constructor; any non-zero time component emits the longer form down to seconds. The value's is ignored - alone decides the frame.

Which frame to build the date in: DateTimeMode.Local for a local-time constructor, DateTimeMode.Utc for a UTC one. This must match how the rest of the page interprets dates or the literal will be off by the client's offset.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.ExpressionCompiler.EscapeJsString(System.String)

summary.para: Turns a CLR string into a safe, quoted JavaScript string literal.

member.param: The text to embed. Its characters are escaped as needed; the result already includes the surrounding quotes, so do not add your own.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.ExpressionCompiler.EscapeJsRegex(System.String)

summary.para: Prepares a regular-expression pattern for use inside a JavaScript regex literal.

member.param: The regex source. Only the characters that would break a slash-delimited literal are adjusted; existing backslash escapes are respected and left intact. The result is the inner pattern text only - wrap it in the slashes (and any flags) yourself.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.ExpressionCompiler.ParameterAccessPath(System.Linq.Expressions.Expression)

summary.para: Builds the dotted member path of a parameter access, for use inside a custom parameter

member.param: The member chain rooted in the parameter (as handed to your function).

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.ExpressionCompiler.Equals(Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.ExpressionCompiler)

summary.para: Tells whether two compilers were built from the same lambda.

member.param: The compiler to compare against; null is never equal.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.ExpressionCompiler.Equals(System.Linq.Expressions.Expression)

summary.para: Tells whether this compiler was built from the given lambda.

member.param: The expression to compare against this compiler's lambda; null is never equal.

summary.para: Gives you the JavaScript runtime that every compiled expression depends on.
Returns the full jsv_* JavaScript runtime stub to embed in your page.
Validation helpers you call from inside the lambdas you compile to JavaScript.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.Functions.Fractional(System.Double)

summary.para: Gets the part of a number after the decimal point, for whole-number or decimal-place checks.

member.param: The number to inspect. The sign is ignored: the magnitude is taken first, so -3.25 yields 0.25 exactly as 3.25 does. An integral value yields 0.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.Functions.DaysSince(System.DateTime,System.DateTime)

summary.para: Counts whole calendar days between two dates, ignoring the time of day.

member.param: The reference (usually later) date; its time component is discarded.
The baseline (usually earlier) date; its time component is discarded. The argument order sets the sign of the result: args0 after args1 gives a positive count, before it gives a negative count.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.Functions.MonthsSince(System.DateTime,System.DateTime)

summary.para: Counts completed calendar months between two dates, ignoring partial months.

member.param: The reference (usually later) date; its time component is discarded.
The baseline (usually earlier) date; its time component is discarded. As with Functions.DaysSince , the argument order sets the sign of the result.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.Functions.YearsSince(System.DateTime,System.DateTime)

summary.para: Counts completed calendar years between two dates - the usual way to check age.

member.param: The reference (usually later) date, e.g. today; its time component is discarded.
The baseline (usually earlier) date, e.g. a birth date; its time component is discarded. The argument order sets the sign of the result.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.Functions.IsCreditCardNumberCorrect(System.String)

summary.para: Validates a payment card number with the Luhn checksum, client and server alike.

member.param: The card number as entered. Embedded spaces and dashes are tolerated (so grouped input like "4111 1111 1111 1111" validates); null or any other non-digit character makes the result false.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.Functions.ToBool(System.Object)

summary.para: Coerces a loosely-typed field to a bool, accepting the common yes/no spellings.

member.param: The value to coerce. null becomes false; an actual passes through; a string is matched case-insensitively - "true"/"yes"/"1"/"on" give true and "false"/"no"/"0"/"off" give false. Any other string, or any other type, is rejected with an exception, so use it only where the input is known to be one of these shapes.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.Functions.ToInt(System.Object)

summary.para: Converts a possibly-textual field to an int for use in numeric rules.

member.param: The value to convert. null becomes 0; an actual passes through; a string is parsed as an integer and throws if it is not a valid one, so guard or pre-validate free text before relying on it. Other types are rejected.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.Functions.IsNull(System.Object)

summary.para: Fluent, translatable stand-in for a null check.

member.param: The value to test; the receiver of the extension call.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.Functions.IsNullOrEmpty(System.String)

summary.para: Fluent "required text" check - true when the string is missing or empty.

member.param: The string to test. Both null and "" yield true; whitespace-only text does not.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.Functions.IsNotNull(System.Object)

summary.para: Fluent, translatable not-null check.

member.param: The value to test; the receiver of the extension call.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.Functions.IsNotNullOrEmpty(System.String)

summary.para: Fluent "text was supplied" check - true when the string is non-empty.

member.param: The string to test. Returns false for both null and "".

summary.para: Translates a constant value into a JavaScript literal.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IConstantTranslator.TryTranslate(System.Object,Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IExpressionEmitContext,System.String@)

summary.para: Implement this to render constants of a type the built-ins don't handle.

member.param: The constant to render; test its type to decide whether to claim it.
Available for escaping strings and reading the date mode while building the literal.
Receives the JavaScript literal when you return true; otherwise leave it null.

summary.para: The building blocks a method-call translator needs to emit JavaScript.
The configured DateTime framing (Local or Utc).
Emit the JavaScript for a sub-expression (walks the tree).
Try to fold a (parameter-free) sub-expression to a constant value.
Emit a lambda as a JavaScript function-expression predicate (function (...) { return ...; }).
Emit access to a parameter-rooted member/indexer chain.
True if the expression is (or roots in) one of the lambda's parameters.
Render a CLR string as a JavaScript string literal.
Escape a regex pattern for use inside a JavaScript /.../ literal.
Additive registry for custom constant types (Guid, enums, value structs, ...).

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IJsConstantRegistry.AddTranslator(Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IConstantTranslator)

summary.para: Register a fully custom constant translator.

member.param: The translator to consult before the built-ins.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IJsConstantRegistry.MapConstant`[c]1(System.Func{[/c]`0,System.String})

summary.para: Register a constant type by giving the function that renders it.

member.param: The constant type this mapping applies to (and its subtypes).
Produces the JavaScript literal for a value of ; it owns any required escaping.

summary.para: Additive registry for custom member/property access.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IJsMemberRegistry.AddTranslator(Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IMemberTranslator)

summary.para: Register a fully custom member translator.

member.param: The translator to consult before the built-ins.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IJsMemberRegistry.MapMember(System.Type,System.String,System.String)

summary.para: Map a property (by declaring type + name) to a JavaScript template.

member.param: The type that declares the property to match.
The property name to match, exactly.
The JavaScript template; $obj is replaced by the emitted target the property is read from.

summary.para: Additive extension surface for mapping method calls to JavaScript.
Register a custom translator (consulted before built-ins).
Map a method (by type + name) to a JavaScript template ($obj, $0, $1, ...).
Map a specific method to a JavaScript template ($obj, $0, $1, ...).
Registry for binding a parameter type to a host-side lookup instead of emitting it verbatim.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IJsParameterRegistry.MapReference(System.Func{System.Type,System.Boolean})

summary.para: Bind the matching rule parameters to the built-in reference() convention.

member.param: Tells whether a rule parameter of the given type should bind to a reference.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IJsParameterRegistry.Map(System.Func{System.Type,System.Boolean},System.Func{System.Linq.Expressions.ParameterExpression,System.String},System.Func{System.Linq.Expressions.Expression,System.Linq.Expressions.ParameterExpression,System.String})

summary.para: Bind the matching rule parameters with custom rendering.

member.param: Tells whether a rule parameter of the given type should bind.
Renders the whole model - the bare parameter. The gives its type (and name) - for example p => "value", or p => "reference('" + p.Type.Name + "')".

Renders a field access, given the member/index chain and the root parameter it is rooted in. Use ExpressionCompiler.ParameterAccessPath to turn the chain into a dotted path - for example (e, p) => "value." + ExpressionCompiler.ParameterAccessPath(e) turns m.Address.PostalCode into value.Address.PostalCode.

summary.para: Translates a member/property access into JavaScript.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IMemberTranslator.TryTranslate(System.Linq.Expressions.MemberExpression,Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IExpressionEmitContext,System.String@)

summary.para: Implement this to emit a property/field read the built-ins don't cover.

member.param: The member access to inspect; its Member and Expression tell you what is being read and from what.
Use it to emit the target expression (member.Expression) and read the date mode.
Receives the emitted JavaScript when you return true; otherwise leave it null.

summary.para: Translates a specific family of method calls into JavaScript.

member.name: M:Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IMethodCallTranslator.TryTranslate(System.Linq.Expressions.MethodCallExpression,Gehtsoft.ExpressionToJs.IExpressionEmitContext,System.String@)

summary.para: Implement this to claim a method call and produce its JavaScript.

member.param: The call to inspect; check its method, declaring type, and arguments to decide whether it is yours.
Use it to emit sub-expressions, fold constants, escape strings, and read the date mode - so your output composes with the rest of the compiler.
Receives the emitted JavaScript when you return true; otherwise leave it null.
